features.summary: Apple reported record Q2 FY2021 results with revenue of $89.6 billion, up 54% year-over-year, driven by strong performance across all product categories. iPhone revenue reached $47.9 billion (up 66%), Services hit a record $16.9 billion (up 27%), and Mac achieved an all-time record of $9.1 billion (up 70%). The company set March quarter records in every geographic segment and saw strong demand for the iPhone 12 family, M1-powered Macs, and continued growth in paid subscriptions to 660 million.
features.management_outlook: Management expects strong double-digit revenue growth in Q3 but warns of a steeper sequential decline than usual due to iPhone 12's later launch timing and supply constraints of $3-4 billion primarily affecting iPad and Mac. They anticipate being supply-gated rather than demand-gated, with semiconductor shortages on legacy nodes being the main constraint. The company raised its dividend by 7% and authorized an additional $90 billion in share repurchases, reflecting confidence in long-term prospects.
features.risks: Primary risks include ongoing semiconductor supply constraints affecting iPad and Mac production, with $3-4 billion expected revenue impact in Q3. COVID-19 uncertainty continues globally with new variants causing lockdowns in some regions. Regulatory scrutiny around App Store practices and services business remains a concern. The company faces potential challenges as pandemic-driven demand trends normalize and consumers potentially shift spending priorities.
features.key_drivers: Strong iPhone 12 family adoption with 5G capabilities driving upgrades and new customer acquisition, with record upgrader numbers for a March quarter. Work-from-home and remote learning trends boosting Mac and iPad demand, supported by M1 chip innovation. Services growth accelerated due to expanding installed base, 660 million paid subscriptions, and recovery in previously affected categories like AppleCare and advertising. Geographic strength was broad-based with particular momentum in China and Europe.
